financial.note: Affirmed 'A-(tha)' with a Stable Outlook in January 2026; ratings are anchored by the group's consolidated credit profile and strong operational linkages.

financial.relation: Strategic Banking Partner / Former JV Partner
financial.note: Maintains a long-standing relationship for funding and credit-provision consultancy, though SAWAD has moved toward consolidated control of its Fast Money subsidiary.
Holds 2.40% equity as of March 2026; provides institutional backing and a liquidity floor for one of Thailand's largest title lenders.

supply_chain.note: The group's motorcycle hire-purchase and personal loan engine; SCAP's performance is critical to group profitability and its 'A-(tha)' rating.
The primary physical channel for vehicle title loans and debt collection services; branch efficiency is the main driver of the 2026 cost-to-income target (~50%).
Heavy reliance on corporate bond issuances; SAWAD is sensitive to shifts in market confidence and domestic interest rates for refinancing its 2026-2027 debt maturities.

competitors.note: Primary rival for the #1 spot in the asset title loan (ATL) market; competition in yield and loan-to-value (LTV) ratios directly pressures SAWAD's NIM.
Direct competition in vehicle title loans and insurance brokerage; TIDLOR's tech-led approach pressures SAWAD's traditional branch-heavy OpEx.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: As a wholesale-funded lender, lower rates in 2026 support margin expansion as funding costs drop faster than lending yields.
Remains elevated at 87% in 2026; high debt levels limit the addressable market for new credit and increase NPL risks for lower-income borrowers.
Strong correlation with the repayment ability of rural customers; 2026 price stability supports lower credit costs for provincial branches.
